title: 5 AFT Recalls and Error Corrections
body_text:
5	AFT Recalls and Error Corrections
5.1	Overview of AFT Recalls and Error Corrections
The difference between a Recall and an Error Correction is that a Recall is used to return an AFT transaction or an AFT file that has not yet been posted to the recipientГўВҖВҷs account(s). An Error Correction is used to reverse an AFT transaction or an AFT file that has been posted to the recipientГўВҖВҷs account(s) in error. A Recall is not an AFT transaction. It is simply a request to return an unposted transaction.
Use ServiceNow to initiate Recall and Error Correction requests. If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete the AFT Recall and Error Correction Request, Form 3295, and send it to Central 1.
Recalls and Error Corrections are subject to service charges. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Contact Central 1 for assistance with Recalls or Error Corrections.
AFT Desk
Phone:
604-737-5962
or
1-800-661-6813
ext.
5962
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
CPA Rule A6, Items in Dispute
CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions
CPA Standard 005
5.2	Recall Deadlines, Restrictions, and Processing
Consider the following when initiating Recalls
for
AFT transactions or AFT files:
Recalls are processed on a best-efforts basis.
Individual debit transactions cannot be recalled if Central 1 has forwarded the transaction to the Receiving Financial Institution.
Credit transactions can be recalled if they have not been posted to the recipientГўВҖВҷs account(s) and the request is received on the business day before the Due Date.
An AFT file can only be recalled if the file has not been forwarded to the data centres of the Receiving Financial Institutions. If an AFT file has been posted to the recipientГўВҖВҷs account(s), the Originator can request an Error Correction.
Financial institutions authorize and submit Recall requests to Central 1 on behalf of their AFT Originators.
Central 1 handles Recall requests as follows:
File Recalls
If Central 1 receives the Recall request by 2:00 pm local time, two business days before the Due Date, Central 1 will delete the file and it will not be forwarded to the receiving data centres. Transactions in the recalled AFT file will not be reported or settled since the original file was not processed through Central 1ГўВҖВҷs AFT system.
If the financial institution wants to Recall a file after Central 1 sends the file to the data centres of the Receiving Financial Institutions but before the transactions are posted to the recipientsГўВҖВҷ accounts, the request is processed as multiple Transaction Recalls.
Transaction Recalls
If Central 1 receives the Recall request before 10:30 am local time the business day before the Due Date, Central 1 will attempt to recall the transaction before it is delivered to the data centre of the Receiving Financial Institution.
Recalled transactions appear on the ICRR/UCRR report as return code 903 ГўВҖВ“ Payment Stopped/Recalled. Settlement is automatically posted to the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs account. See the AFT Reports Part for more information on the ICRR/UCRR Report.
If the Recall request is successful, it is generally settled to the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs account automatically.
5.3	Error Correction Deadlines, Restrictions, and Processing
If an AFT transaction or an AFT file was posted to the recipientsГўВҖВҷ account(s) in error, the financial institution, on behalf of the AFT Originator, can request an Error Correction.
Consider the following when initiating Error Corrections for AFT transactions or AFT files:
Error Corrections are processed on a best-efforts basis.
Error Corrections do not guarantee the permanent return of funds since they can be disputed within 90 days of the posting. If disputed after this time, the matter is handled outside of the Clearing.
Original AFT transactions are still eligible for return despite Error Correction requests. AFT returns will be posted to the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs account. If there is a disagreement brought forward by the Originator, the financial institution can choose to handle it as an Item in Dispute or have the Originator handle the issue outside of the Clearing with its customer.
Financial institutions authorize and submit Error Correction requests to Central 1 on behalf of their Originators.
Note:	Payments Canada prohibits the use of Error Corrections to protect financial institutions from insolvent Originators.
Note:	Error Corrections cannot be requested for returned AFT transactions (I & J records).
The deadline for Error Corrections is 2:00 pm local time, 3 business days following the Settlement Date.
Central 1 creates the Error Correction transactions and forwards them to the other financial institutions. Original Credit (C) transactions are reversed with Debit (E) transactions and original Debit (D) transactions are reversed with Credit (F) transactions. Check the Summary of Reversed Error Corrections Report (ICSE/UCSE) for details.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on this report.
The financial institutionГўВҖВҷs account with Central 1 will be credited or debited automatically. Settlement will be posted to the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs financial institution account.
5.4	Initiating AFT Recalls and Error Corrections in ServiceNow
Proceed as follows to initiate an AFT Recall or Error Correction in ServiceNow.
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login

Applications
, then select
Production Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre

ServiceNow

Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow

Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.

Electronic Payments
and then select

Complete the AFT Recall and Error Correction Request page as follows. See Figure 6, Figure 7, and Figure 8.

Complete the File Details section or the Transaction Details section, as applicable:
AFT File
ГўВҖВ“ if a Recall or Error Correction is requested for an AFT file, complete the File Details section. The fields in this section are the same regardless of whether you request a File Recall or File Error Correction. The File Details section does not appear for single or multiple Transaction Recalls or transaction Error Corrections. See Figure 6.
Single AFT Transaction
ГўВҖВ“ if a Recall or Error Correction is requested for a single AFT transaction, complete the Transaction Details section. The fields in this section are the same regardless of whether you request a Transaction Recall or a transaction Error Correction. The Transaction Details section does not appear for File Recalls or File Error Corrections. See Figure 7.
Multiple Transactions
ГўВҖВ“ if a Recall or Error Correction is requested for multiple transactions, proceed as follows:
ГўВҲВ’
Select the

link to complete the spreadsheet. The fields in the spreadsheet are the same as those displayed in the Transaction Details section for single transactions.
ГўВҲВ’
Save the file and then select the
Click here to attach
link to upload the file to ServiceNow. Follow the prompts. See Figure 8.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Enter additional comments if necessary.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select

. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 6: Recall or Error Correction ГўВҖВ“ File

5.5 	Initiating AFT Recalls or Error Corrections Manually
If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete the AFT Recall and Error Correction Request, Form 3295, to initiate a Recall or Error Correction. Central 1 will only accept this form if ServiceNow is unavailable. For information on Recall and Error Correction deadlines, restrictions, and processing details, see Section 5.2, Recall Deadlines, Restrictions, and Processing, and Section 5.3, Error Correction Deadlines, Restrictions, and Processing.
Proceed as follows:
Complete the AFT Recall and Error Correction Request, Form 3295. Completion of the form is self-explanatory but note the following. See Figure 9.
ГўВ‘
File Details
ГўВҖВ“ Complete this section to initiate a Recall or Error Correction request for an AFT file.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Transaction Details
ГўВҖВ“ Complete this section to initiate a Recall or Error Correction request for an AFT transaction. Complete page 2 to submit a request for multiple transactions.
Figure 9: AFT Recall and Error Correction Request, Form 3295
Have the form signed by one signing officer on file with Central 1.
Send the form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Related Information
AFT Recall and Error Correction Request,
Form 3295
5.6	Receiving AFT Recalls
Some financial institutions use the term ГўВҖВңRecallГўВҖВқ to see individual Error Correction transactions or to trace and return requests from other financial institutions. However, CPA Rules stipulate that Recalls cannot be processed after the business day prior to the Due Date. As such, an incoming Recall, as defined by the CPA, will not be known to the financial institution. The transaction will be warehoused at Central 1 and will not be posted to the memberГўВҖВҷs account. Neither financial institutions nor their members will be aware of AFT Recalls initiated by the Originating Direct Clearer.
Related Information
Section 6.4
, Receiving an AFT Trace Request
5.7	Receiving AFT Error Corrections
The Originator of an AFT file from another financial institution can initiate a file or a transaction Error Correction. An original Credit (C) transaction is reversed with a Debit (E) transaction and an original Debit (D) transaction is reversed with a Credit (F) transaction. The Error Correction transactions are automatically posted to the membersГўВҖВҷ accounts. Check the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing Report (CCD/UCD) for details.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on this report.
Financial institutions are required to post transactions even if debits, which are received to reverse an original credit, are not normally accepted for non-chequing accounts. However, a Payor/Payee can refuse an Error Correction provided it is done within 90 days from the posting date. See the AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions Part for more information.
END
